---
title: "Discovery of a New Mammoth Lineage in Mexico"
format: html
date: 2025-08-28
image: cover/mammut_colombino.jpg
---

::: video-card
UNAM specialists carried out the first genetic study of mammoths in Mexico, uncovering unique mitochondrial lineages and publishing their findings in [*Science*](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adt9651).

<div style="text-align:center;">
  <iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/H9VpCuroeXg?start=13" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen>

  </iframe>
</div>
:::

### Summary

-   **Groundbreaking research**: UNAM specialists carried out the first genetic study of megafauna in Mexico, analyzing mammoth remains found during the construction of the Felipe Ángeles International Airport.

-   **Key finding**: Mammoths from the Basin of Mexico show **distinct mitochondrial lineages** compared to those reported in the U.S. and Canada, revealing a more complex evolutionary history.

-   **International recognition**: Results were published in *Science*, positioning Mexican paleogenomics as a globally relevant discipline.

-   **Methodology**: DNA was recovered from more than 80 mammoth molars using advanced capture-enrichment techniques, successfully sequencing 61 mitochondrial genomes.

-   **Implications**: The study suggests that the Columbian mammoth species originated through hybridization between steppe and woolly mammoths, and that Mexican specimens diverged from northern ones over 400,000 years ago.

-   **Collaboration**: An interdisciplinary project involving UNAM, INAH, and ENAH, with active participation of students and young researchers.

-   **Regional importance**: The Santa Lucía fossil collection is the largest in Latin America, with more than 70,000 megafauna remains.

👉 Source: [UNAM Global – Especialistas de la UNAM descubren nuevo linaje de mamut](https://unamglobal.unam.mx/global_tv/especialistas-de-la-unam-descubren-nuevo-linaje-de-mamut/)
